food_subgroup: Lebanese herb and bulgur wheat salad — fresh flat-leaf parsley, fine bulgur wheat, mint, tomato, spring onion, lemon juice, and extra virgin olive oil

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tabbouleh as a specific food item; not a clinical substance requiring SNOMED coding.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of tabbouleh is approximately 150g, equivalent to a generous side-salad portion as served in Lebanese and Middle Eastern restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Tabbouleh — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID: 172957)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy contains a direct entry for tabbouleh. Values for per_100g macros, micronutrients, and minerals are sourced from this entry. Vitamin K is notably high in tabbouleh due to the predominance of flat-leaf parsley; estimated ~400mcg vitamin K per 100g based on parsley content.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; bulgur wheat GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~45 is based on the well-established GI of bulgur wheat (cracked and parboiled wheat) from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021). Bulgur wheat GI ranges 46–53 in published tables; tabbouleh GI is moderated further by the high parsley content, olive oil fat, and lemon juice acidity, supporting a GI estimate of ~45. GL per 150g serving = (45 × 12g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.5) / 100 = 8.1; adjusted to 6.3 reflecting the net available carbohydrate after fibre subtraction (net carb ~9.3g per 100g × 1.5 × 45 / 100 = 6.3). Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~200mg per 150g serving); extra virgin olive oil polyphenols with mild antihypertensive effect
hypertension.guidance: Tabbouleh is one of the lowest-sodium salads available in Middle Eastern cuisine at approximately 133mg sodium per 100g (~200mg per 150g serving), making it highly suitable for hypertensive patients; the extra virgin olive oil contributes hydroxytyrosol and oleuropein polyphenols associated with modest systolic blood pressure reduction (~3 mmHg) in Mediterranean diet adherence studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~45) and low GL (~6.3 per 150g serving); dietary fibre and olive oil fat moderate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tabbouleh has a low glycemic index of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of approximately 6.3 per 150g serving, making it an excellent choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the combination of bulgur wheat dietary fibre (2.7g per 100g), olive oil monounsaturated fat, and lemon juice acidity collectively attenuate postprandial glucose excursion, and it is one of the most glycaemically favourable accompaniments in Middle Eastern restaurant dining.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High MUFA from extra virgin olive oil; very low saturated fat (0.5g per 100g); zero trans fat; high folate and potassium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tabbouleh is cardioprotective: extra virgin olive oil provides monounsaturated fatty acids (~2.2g per 100g) consistent with Mediterranean diet cardiovascular benefit, saturated fat is very low at 0.5g per 100g, trans fat is zero, and the high folate content (~120mcg per 100g) supports homocysteine metabolism; the dish is suitable for regular consumption in a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory olive oil polyphenols; high folate critical for methotrexate patients; HIGH VITAMIN K — warfarin interaction
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tabbouleh is strongly positive for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to anti-inflammatory olive oil polyphenols and high folate (~180mcg per 150g serving, critical for patients on methotrexate); however, the very high vitamin K content (~400mcg per 100g from flat-leaf parsley) requires consistent portion control for patients on warfarin anticoagulation therapy to avoid INR destabilisation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tabbouleh (تبولة) is a traditional Lebanese herb salad and one of the most iconic dishes of Middle Eastern cuisine, originating from Lebanon and Syria. Authentic Lebanese tabbouleh is predominantly flat-leaf parsley — the parsley-to-grain ratio is typically 2:1 or higher — with fine bulgur wheat (bourghul), fresh mint, ripe tomato, spring onion, freshly squeezed lemon juice, and extra virgin olive oil. Western adaptations often invert this ratio, making the dish more grain-heavy. The texture is fresh, finely chopped, and herbaceous, with a bright acidic flavour from lemon and a grassy richness from parsley. Per 100g: 89 kcal, 2g protein, 3.3g fat (predominantly MUFA from olive oil), 12g carbohydrate, 2.7g dietary fibre, 133mg sodium. Per 150g serving: ~135 kcal, 3g protein, 5g fat, 18g carbohydrate, 4g fibre, ~200mg sodium. GI ~45 (low), GL ~6.3 per serving. CLINICAL ALERTS: (1) Very high vitamin K — approximately 400mcg per 100g from flat-leaf parsley; patients on warfarin must maintain consistent portion sizes to avoid INR destabilisation. (2) High folate (~120mcg per 100g, ~180mcg per 150g serving) — clinically significant for patients on methotrexate (rheumatoid arthritis). Tabbouleh is one of the most nutritionally positive foods available in Middle Eastern restaurant dining: low GI, very low sodium, zero cholesterol, high folate, and anti-inflammatory olive oil polyphenols. Source: USDA SR Legacy. Confidence: high.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy, which contains a dedicated tabbouleh entry. GI value of ~45 is well-supported by published bulgur wheat GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent. Ontology anchors (FoodOn exact match, FoodEx2 direct match) are defensible.
notes: Vitamin B12 (0.0 mcg per 100g) is confirmed zero as tabbouleh is a plant-based dish containing no animal products; this is naturally zero. Vitamin D (0.0 mcg per 100g) is naturally zero as this is a plant-based salad with no animal-derived or UV-exposed ingredients. Cholesterol (0.0 mg per 100g) is naturally zero as the dish contains no animal products. Trans fat (0.0g per 100g) is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. Monounsaturated fat (2.2g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.4g) are estimated from the olive oil content of tabbouleh based on USDA SR Legacy olive oil fatty acid profile applied to the fat content of the dish. Water_g set to -1: moisture data for the composite tabbouleh dish as prepared is not separately reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry consulted. CLINICAL NOTE: Flat-leaf parsley is exceptionally high in vitamin K (~1640mcg per 100g raw parsley per USDA); in tabbouleh where parsley is the dominant ingredient, vitamin K per 100g of the dish is estimated at approximately 400mcg, and per 150g serving approximately 600mcg — patients on warfarin anticoagulation must be counselled on consistent consumption. High folate (~120mcg per 100g) is clinically significant for patients on methotrexate. FoodEx2 code A0GSF used as provided in input; verified as a plausible FoodEx2 code for this food category. SNOMED CT code not available for tabbouleh as a specific food item.
